macro_segments.title: Forced Get (Get Me'useh): Validity Based on Source of Compulsion and Legal Mandate

micro_segments.title: Mishnah: Get Me'useh BiYisrael Kasher - Forced Get Valid When Compelled by Jewish Court
Rav Nachman's Ruling: Legal Mandate Required for Validity; No Legal Mandate Creates Re'ach Haget Problem
Rambam's Explanation: Overcoming Yetzer Hara and True Free Will in Forced Get
Four-Case Matrix: Jewish Court + Legal Mandate (Valid); Jewish Court Without (Re'ach Haget); Non-Jewish Court With Mandate (Re'ach Haget); Non-Jewish Court Without (No Problem)
Rav Mesharshaya's Extreme Position: Non-Jewish Court with Legal Mandate is Biblically Valid (Rejected as Absurd)

macro_segments.title: Authority to Force Get: Semicha Requirements and Common Case (Milsa DeShchicha) Exception

micro_segments.title: Abaye Challenges Rav Yosef: Lack of Semicha Should Disqualify Court from Forcing Get
Rabbi Tarfon's Teaching: Non-Jewish Courts Forbidden Even with Same Substantive Law
Rav Yosef's Response: Court Representatives Act as Agents in Common Cases (Milsa DeShchicha)
Gemara's Pushback: Why Not Agents for Theft and Personal Injury? Distinction of Common vs. Uncommon Cases
Tosafot's Questions on Semicha: Converting Gerim and Agency of Ancient Courts That No Longer Exist

macro_segments.title: Rumors About Marriage Status (Kol): Narrow Conditions for Halakhic Concern and Discharging Rumors

micro_segments.title: Mishnah: Rumor of Marriage Makes Woman Forbidden; Rumor of Divorce Allows Remarriage; Explanation (Amatla) Neutralizes
Rav Assi Limits Mishna: Only Single Woman Concerned About Marriage Rumor; Married Woman Not Affected
Raba: Rumor of Promiscuity (Mezanah) Not Concerning - Frivolous Gossip Based on Minor Impropriety
Tannaim Debate on Evidence: Rabbi Meir vs. Rabbi Akiva on How Easily Evidence Establishes Adultery
Rabbi Yochanan ben Nuri's Objection: Require Two Witnesses for Serious Claims, Not Mere Rumors

macro_segments.title: Severely Limiting Kol: Specific Conditions and Types of Rumors That Create No Concern

micro_segments.title: Baraitah List: No Concern for Rumors of Sex, Marriage, Betrothal, Unnamed Person, Another City, Mamzeret, Shifcha
Ulla's Standard: Rumor Only Concerning When Circumstantial Evidence (Candles, Beds, People Entering/Exiting) Present
Requirement of 'Nitkadshah': Rumor Must State 'Got Married' Not 'Getting Married' (Established Fact vs. Plan)
Rav Papa & Rav Huna: Alternative Track - Identifying Direct Witnesses Who Aren't Present (Tracing Rumor Source)

macro_segments.title: Proactive Nullification of Rumors (Bittul Kol): Conditions for Court Intervention and Effectiveness

micro_segments.title: Abaye's Question: Should Beis Din Proactively Work to Dispel Rumors or Leave Them Alone?
Rav Chisda vs. Rav Sheshet: Debate on Stringency - Must Witnesses Be Kosher or Can Women Also Establish Kol?
Regional Practice: Sura Nullifies Rumors; Nehardea Does Not - Based on Rav and Shmuel's Positions
Case of Conditional Marriage: Later Explanation Does Not Nullify Already-Established Rumor
Dates Case: Effective Nullification When Rumor's Own Logic Provides Explanation (Unripe Dates)
Unnamed Son Case: Nullify When Explanation Fits Within Rumor (One Son Was Minor)
Mature-Looking Minor: Hesitation to Nullify When Explanation Contradicts Perception

macro_segments.title: Amatla (Explanation): Timing and Sufficiency for Neutralizing Established Rumors

micro_segments.title: Rabba bar Rav Huna: Explanation Valid Even Ten Days Later (Lenient Position)
Rav Zvid: Possibility of Amatla Sufficient to Neutralize Rumor (Even Hypothetical Explanation)
Case of Conditional Kiddushin: Woman Allowed to Return to First Husband Because Conditional Marriage Presumed
Gemara's Distinction: Explanation Efficacy Depends on Whether Husband Contests and Reveals True Circumstances
Nisuin Creates Issur (Violation): Different Standard When Woman Has Already Lived with Second Husband

Rav Chisda vs. Rav Sheshet (Witness Requirements)
Machzir Gerushaso (Taking Back Divorced Wife - Prohibited)
